task_completion.description: Open the provided 3D scene in Blender.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender application is open, and the 3D viewport displays the BMW car model scene.
A 'Blender Render' window is open, showing the scene actively rendering (with progress indicators/tiles) or the fully rendered image of the BMW car.

task_completion.description: Open the dialog to save the rendered image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save As' or 'Save Image' file browser dialog is open within Blender, typically overlaid on the render window.
The filename field in the save dialog is explicitly set to 'rendered_output.png'.

task_completion.description: Save the output to the specified BlenderProjects directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The directory path in the save dialog is set to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/' before saving, or a subsequent file manager/terminal window confirms the file 'rendered_output.png' exists in this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender GUI to render the scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Blender graphical interface (e.g., clicking 'Render -> Render Image' or using the UI save dialog) rather than executing a headless background terminal command.

integrity.description: The scene was actually rendered by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender Render window displays rendering statistics (such as Time, Mem, Peak, or visible rendering tiles) at the top left, proving the image was computed and not just opened from an existing file.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass rendering by copying a pre-existing image.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a clear progression from opening the .blend file to rendering it and saving it. The agent does not open a web browser to download a BMW render or copy an image from another directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target scene is the official BMW27 Blender Benchmark Scene (featuring a BMW car). The expected output file must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/rendered_output.png'. Note: While the base resolution in the Blender file is 1920x1080, a 50% resolution scaling factor is applied in the settings, meaning the final rendered image will strictly be 960x540. The final file size should be at least 50 KB (typically around 636 KB).
